description: 42F with LDL 230 mg/dL. Severe hypercholesterolemia triggers high-intensity statin per ACC/AHA Section 4.2.

actions.label: Initiate maximally tolerated high-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE B-NR: adults aged 20-75 with LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL should be treated with maximally tolerated high-intensity statin. LDL 230 mg/dL exceeds the ≥190 threshold. No ASCVD risk calculation required at this LDL level. Likely familial hypercholesterolemia given LDL ≥190 in a 42-year-old without secondary causes.

actions.label: Consider evaluation for familial hypercholesterolemia
actions.rationale: LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL in a young adult raises suspicion for familial hypercholesterolemia. Genetic testing and cascade screening of first-degree relatives may be warranted.

actions.label: Lifestyle modification counseling (diet, exercise)
actions.rationale: Therapeutic lifestyle changes are foundational alongside statin therapy. Heart-healthy diet, regular physical activity, and weight management support LDL reduction.

contraindications.label: Moderate-intensity statin as sole therapy
contraindications.rationale: LDL ≥190 mg/dL requires high-intensity statin per ACC/AHA. Moderate-intensity alone is insufficient for this degree of hypercholesterolemia.

contraindications.label: Defer statin pending ASCVD risk calculation
contraindications.rationale: ACC/AHA explicitly states that LDL ≥190 warrants high-intensity statin without requiring ASCVD risk calculation.
